headline: Laura Jackson: cool girls go on cruises
summary: The creative director sets sail on a family trip around the British Isles
body_text:
This summer I went on my first cruise – a 12-day trip around the British Isles. My dad was a super-keen cruiser. He and my stepmum had travelled all around the world. My dad was unwell, so it was our last opportunity to travel together; he passed away earlier this month.

My typical holiday always looks like an adventure. For our last couple of trips, I organised a house swap in Ballymaloe, Ireland and to Dalí’s Cadaqués. As someone who works in the food and lifestyle space, I take great joy in finding the best markets and neighbourhood restaurants, so being on a cruise where everything is packaged for you was completely out of my comfort zone.

Our ship Princess left Southampton with around 3,500 passengers (3,495 of whom I didn’t know) across 19 decks. At times, I felt like I was stuck in the Trafford Centre with the tannoys yelling “hellooooo, Cruisers” (in a thick American twang), or in the “Feed Me!” scene from Darren Aronofsky’s Requiem For a Dream. There was no escaping the peop
